response:
THE CENTRAL CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS OF R CM. THE NEXT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS THAT IS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS ONE, AND THIS PATTERN CONTINUES FOR A TOTAL OF 8 CONCENTRIC CIRCLES. CALCULATE THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES COMBINED. EXPRESS YOUR ANSWER IN TERMS OF R AND PI.

THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS πR² + π(1.1R)² + π(1.21R)² + π(1.331R)² + π(1.4641R)² + π(1.61051R)² + π(1.771561R)² + π(1.9487171R)².

TO ADD A TOUCH OF TRADITIONAL TYPOGRAPHY, THE DESIGNER DECIDES TO PLACE TEXT ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE. IF THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS USED TO EVENLY SPACE TEXT CHARACTERS SUCH THAT THERE IS A 0.5 CM GAP BETWEEN EACH CHARACTER, AND THE TOTAL NUMBER OF CHARACTERS (INCLUDING SPACES) MUST BE AN INTEGER, DETERMINE THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE.

THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THIS CIRCUMFERENCE IS 110.
